subtitle: The Açoteias end of the Algarve's six-kilometre wall of red-ochre cliffs, where five million years of stacked sediment stand cross-sectioned above the sand.
spike_statement: A nearly 6 km run of cliffs banded white-to-red, the upper Quarteira Sands stained ochre by iron oxide, recording roughly five million years of deposition.

levers.photo_tone: iron-oxide red and terracotta cliff bands above pale sand, pine-green crowns, Atlantic blue water in the lower frame
